title: dryopteris punctata subsp. rugosula
intro: Dryopteris punctata subsp. rugosula, also known as the prickly fern, is a subspecies of the Dryopteris punctata plant, a type of fern native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the prickly fern are often dried and used in teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
